title: Generation of Human Neutrophils from Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells Using ETV2 Modified mRNA

steps.title: Transfection of ETV2 Modified mRNA (mmRNA) into hiPSCs and Induction of Hemogenic Endothelial Lineage
Transfection of ETV2 Modified mRNA (mmRNA) into hiPSCs and Induction of Hemogenic Endothelial Lineage
steps.instruction: To induce hemogenic endothelium, hiPSCs are transfected with ETV2¬†mmRNA in TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media using TransIT reagent and mRNA boost.28.On day 0, retrieve collagen IV-coated 6-well plate from 4¬įC storage and keep at 37¬įC for at least 30¬†min before starting the differentiation of iPSCs.29.Aspirate the collagen and put 2¬†mL prewarmed TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media supplemented with 10¬†őľM Y-27632 Dihydrochloride into each well.30.When iPSCs are 60% in confluency after 3¬†days of culture (Figure¬†3), aspirate the media and add 1¬†mL prewarmed HyQTase for single cell suspension of the iPSCs (Figure¬†4A).

steps.title: Transfection of ETV2 Modified mRNA (mmRNA) into hiPSCs and Induction of Hemogenic Endothelial Lineage
steps.instruction: Note: Single cell suspension requires HyQTase instead of EDTA/PBS solution. EDTA/PBS does not make cells into single cell suspension. It only maintains the cells as clumps which is required for passaging of hiPSCs. But for differentiation, it needs single cell suspension.31.Incubate at 37¬įC, 5% CO2, incubator for 3¬†min.CRITICAL: Incubation should not exceed more than 3¬†min and should be optimized for each type of cell line. After 3¬†min of incubation you can observe single cells in the plate under the microscope. HyClone‚ĄĘ HyQTase‚ĄĘ solution has since been discontinued by GE Healthcare Life Sciences, therefore, we recommend to use Accutase¬ģ (Innovative Cell Technologies, Inc., Cat. #AT-104) instead.32.Add 1¬†mL of prewarmed TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media into each well.33.Collect the cells into a 15¬†mL conical tube and centrifuge at 350√óg for 5¬†min at room temperature and aspirate the supernatant.CRITICAL: Do not aspirate the HyClone‚ĄĘ HyQTase‚ĄĘ solution from the plate.Optional: HyClone‚ĄĘ HyQTase‚ĄĘ has since been discontinued by GE Healthcare Life Sciences. We therefore recommend to use Accutase¬ģ (Innovative Cell Technologies, Inc., Cat. #AT-104) instead.34.To remove residual HyQTase‚ĄĘ, resuspend the cells in 2¬†mL of TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media.35.Centrifuge at 350√óg for 5¬†min at room temperature and aspirate the supernatant.36.Resuspend the cells in 1¬†mL of TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media.37.Determine cell viability by trypan blue staining and a hemocytometer.38.Plate 2√ó105 cells into each well of a 6-well plate containing 2¬†mL of TeSR‚ĄĘ-E8‚ĄĘ complete media with 10¬†őľM Rock inhibitor and gently rock the plate side-to-side and back-and-forth to spread the cells evenly across the well.CRITICAL: Cell number should be highly accurate as transfection efficiency is highly dependent on cell density and viability.39.Incubate the plate at 37¬įC, 5% CO2, for 30¬†min to adhere the cells.40.Place the TransIT reagent and mRNA boost (TransIT¬ģ-mRNA Kit, Mirus) and ETV2 mmRNA aliquot on ice (Figure¬†4B).41.Prepare ETV2 mmRNA transfection master mix in a sterile 1.5¬†mL microcentrifuge tube.42.Mix the components by gentle pipetting and add the ‚ąľ100¬†őľL transfection mixture to each well of the 6-well plate (Figure¬†4C).CRITICAL: Add the transfection master mix drop by drop into each well evenly. Rock the plate after addition to properly distribute.43.Place in 37¬įC, 5% CO2, incubator for 24 h.CRITICAL: We recommend not to use an iPSC cell line beyond passage 30, after which, hematopoietic differentiation capacity reduces.Figure 4Preparation of Cell Culture Hood Set up for Single Cell Suspension and Transfection Procedure(A) Suggested cell culture hood set up for the single cell suspension of hiPSCs.(B) Recommended cell culture hood set up for the transfection of the ETV2 mmRNA transfection.(C) Addition of the ETV2 mmRNA master mix in to the respective wells of hiPSCs.

steps.title: Flow Cytometric Analysis of Myeloid Progenitors and Neutrophils
Flow Cytometric Analysis of Myeloid Progenitors and Neutrophils
steps.instruction: Flow cytometric analysis is used to identify the population of interest among a mixture of different populations.67.Cell suspension to a concentration of 1√ó106 cells/mL in flow cytometry buffer. Cells are usually stained in polystyrene round bottom falcon tubes.68.Add 0.1‚Äď10¬†őľg/mL conjugated primary antibody.Note: Dilutions should be made in flow cytometry buffer (Ghost dye‚ĄĘ Violet 540 can also be added at this point for dead cell exclusion).69.Incubate for at least 30¬†min in dark at 4¬įC.70.Wash the cells by centrifugation at 350√óg for 5¬†min and resuspend them in 500¬†őľL of flow cytometry buffer.71.Analyze the cells on the flow cytometer.
